feed.subtitle: A stash of notes and observations on the experience with Proxmox VE, some of which you might find useful.
feed.title: free-pmx: Insights on Proxmox

entry.title: Upgrades and repositories
entry.summary: Set up necessary APT repositories upon fresh Proxmox VE install without any subscription license. Explainer on apt, apt-get, upgrade, dist-upgrade and full-upgrade.

entry.title: Getting rid of systemd-boot
entry.summary: Ditch the unexpected bootloader from ZFS install on a UEFI system without SecureBoot. Replace it with the more common GRUB and remove superfluous BIOS boot partition.

entry.title: Verbose boot with GRUB
entry.summary: Most PVE boots are entirely quiet. Avoid issues with troubleshooting non-booting system later by setting verbose boots. If you are already in trouble, there is a remedy as well.

entry.title: ZFSBootMenu setup for Proxmox VE
entry.summary: A complete feature-set bootloader for ZFS on root install. It allows booting off multiple datasets, selecting kernels, creating snapshots and clones, rollbacks and much more - as much as a rescue system would.

entry.title: How to disable HA permanently
entry.summary: Do away with the High Availability stack in a safe manner. Avoid the risk of reboots when troubleshooting suspected hardware woes. Particularly useful for frequent loss-of-quorum scenarios.

entry.title: How to disable HA for maintenance
entry.summary: Avoid unexpected non-suspect node reboot during maintenance in any High Availability cluster. No need to wait for any grace periods until it becomes inactive by itself, no uncertainties.

entry.title: DHCP setup of a single node
entry.summary: Set up your sole node Proxmox VE install as any other server - with DHCP assigned IP address. Useful when IPs are managed as static reservations or dynamic environments. No pesky scripting involved.

entry.title: DHCP setup of a cluster
entry.summary: Keep control of the entire cluster pool of IPs from your networking plane. Avoid potential IP conflicts and streamline automated deployments with DHCP managed, albeit statically reserved assignments.

entry.title: Backup Cluster configuration - /etc/pve
entry.summary: Backup cluster-wide configuration virtual filesystem in a safe manner, plan for disaster recovery for the case of corrupt database. A situation more common than anticipated.

entry.title: Extract Cluster configuration - /etc/pve
entry.summary: Recover individual cluster configuration without need for a bogus node. Both from previous backup or a corrupt database. The source does not need to originate from the same cluster.

entry.title: Rescue or backup entire host
entry.summary: Access PVE host root filesystem when booting off Proxmox installer ISO. A non-intuitive case of ZFS install not supported by regular Live Debian. Fast full host backup (no guests) demonstration resulting in 1G archive that is sent out over SSH. This will allow for flexible redeployment in a follow-up guide. No proprietary products involved, just regular Debian tooling.

entry.title: Restore entire host from backup
entry.summary: Restore a full root filesystem of a backed up Proxmox node - use case with ZFS as an example, but can be appropriately adjusted for other systems. Approach without obscure tools. Simple tar, sgdisk and chroot. A follow-up to the previous post on backing up the entire root filesystem offline from a rescue boot.

entry.title: Proxmox VE nag removal, manually
entry.summary: Brief look at what exactly brings up the dreaded notice regarding no valid subscription. Eliminate bad UX that no user of free software should need to endure.

entry.title: Proxmox VE nag removal, scripted
entry.summary: Automate subscription notice suppression to avoid manually intervention during periods of active UI development. No risky scripts with obscure regular expressions that might corrupt the system in the future.

entry.title: Container shell with no password
entry.summary: Do not set passwords on container users, get shell with native LXC tooling taking advantage of the host authentication. Reduce attack surfaces of exposed services.

entry.title: WARNING: Setting locale failed
entry.summary: Common Perl warning during upgrades regarding locale settings lies in AcceptEnv directive of SSH config. A better default for any Proxmox VE install, or any Debian-based server in fact.

entry.title: ERROR: dpkg processing archive during apt install
entry.summary: Conflicts in files as packaged by Proxmox and what finds its way into underlying Debian install do arise. Pass proper options to the apt command for remedy.

entry.title: ERROR: failed to load local private key
entry.summary: Misleading error message during failed boot-up of a cluster node that can send you chasing a red herring. Recognise it and rectify the actual underlying issue.

entry.title: The Proxmox cluster probe
entry.summary: Experimental setup that can in fact serve as a probe to the health of a cluster. Unlike e.g. Quorum Device, it mimics an actual full fledged node without the hardware or architecture requirements.

entry.title: The Proxmox cluster filesystem build
entry.summary: The bespoke filesystem that is the heart of Proxmox stack compiles from its sources in C. Necessary when changing hardcoded defaults or debugging unexplained quirks.

entry.title: Taking advantage of ZFS on root
entry.summary: A look at limited support of ZFS by Proxmox VE stock install. A primer on ZFS basics insofar ZFS as a root filesystem setups - snapshots and clones, with examples. Preparation for ZFS bootloader install with offline backups all-in-one guide.

entry.title: Making sense of Proxmox bootloaders
entry.summary: What is the bootloader setup determined by and why? What is the role of the Proxmox boot tool? Explore the quirks behind the approach of supporting everything.

entry.title: Why Proxmox offer full feature set for free
entry.summary: Everything has its cost. Running off repositories that only went through limited internal testing takes its toll on the user. Be aware of the implications.

entry.title: A glimpse at Proxmox Quality Assurance
entry.summary: What kind of testing procedures do they use at Proxmox and how does your bug-reporting fit into it? How consistent and thorough is regression testing before users get hold of a public package?

entry.title: The 'no-subscription' repository
entry.summary: What exactly do you get from the non-subscriber and freely available package repository? And what are the differences to the one meant for public testing only. Almost none.

entry.title: How Proxmox VE shreds your SSDs
entry.summary: Debug-level look at what exactly is wrong with the crucial component of every single Proxmox node, including non-clustered ones. History of regressions tracked to decisions made during increase of size limits.

entry.title: The pmxcfs mountpoint of /etc/pve
entry.summary: Understand the setup of virtual filesystem that holds cluster-wide configurations and has a not-so-usual behaviour - unlike any other regular filesystem.

entry.title: Some lesser known quorum options
entry.summary: When considering a Quorum Device for small clusters, be aware of other valid alternatives that were taken off the list only due to High Availability stack concerns.

entry.title: The Proxmox Corosync fallacy
entry.summary: Distinguish the role of Corosync in Proxmox clusters from the rest of the stack and appreciate the actual reasons behind unexpected reboots or failed quorums.

entry.title: Improved SSH with hidden regressions
entry.summary: Over 10 years old bug finally got fixed. What changes did it bring and what undocumented regressions to expect? How to check your current install and whether it is affected?

entry.title: Why Proxmox VE shreds your SSDs
entry.summary: Quantify the idle writes of every single Proxmox node that contribute to premature failure of some SSDs despite their high declared endurance.

entry.title: Passwordless SSH can lock you out
entry.summary: Not so intuitive cascade of events may lead to inability to SSH connect to a node with otherwise healthy networking setup due to inaccessible keys location.

entry.title: The Proxmox time bomb watchdog
entry.summary: The unexpected reboot you have encountered might have had nothing to do with any hardware problem. Details on specific Proxmox watchdog setup missing from official documentation.
